lines: I am not lost in the heroic sense. I am misplaced by trees that keep moving when I blink.
Guide me to the old oak, then back toward the west road, and I shall stop giving speeches to mushrooms.
The old oak leans like a librarian. I can almost read the way home from here.
There is the west road. Page Pip will pretend not to worry, which is how I know he did.
My next errand is indoors, near walls with the decency to stay put.
This leaf looks like a map if one has already given up.
